name: Report a Fraudulent Transaction and Get Provisional Credit
description: Call a bank's fraud line to report one or more unauthorized transactions on a debit or credit account, lock or reissue the compromised card, and secure a provisional credit while the investigation runs.
disclaimer: Not financial or tax advice. Agent must NEVER share SSN unprompted, NEVER initiate wires above the operator's stated cap, NEVER agree to a hardship plan / settlement that affects credit without explicit ask_operator approval.

context.when_to_use: User sees one or more charges they did not authorize on a checking, savings, or credit card statement. Call promptly — Regulation E gives the strongest protection when reported within 2 business days for debit cards, and Reg Z caps unauthorized credit-card liability at $50 regardless. Use the fraud number printed on the back of the card or the bank's official site, NOT a number from an email or text.
preconditions: User has the account number or card number (last 4 is fine to start).
User can list each fraudulent transaction: date, amount, merchant string.
User has confirmed the card is in their possession (or knows it is missing).
User has NOT clicked any links in 'fraud alert' texts/emails — those are often the scam itself.
Operator-stated cap on any actions that move money or change product terms.

principles: Speed matters legally. Reg E protection drops sharply after 2 business days for debit and after 60 days for credit. Open the report on this call, even if details are incomplete.

Use ONLY the number on the back of the card or the bank's official website. If the user got a 'fraud alert' text, do not call the number in it — call the card-back number and ask 'did you flag activity on my account?'

Separate 'card compromised' from 'identity compromised'. One bad debit charge usually means card data was skimmed; a new account opened in the user's name means identity theft and a different workflow (credit freeze, IdentityTheft.gov report).
Provisional credit is your goal on this call. Reg E requires it within 10 business days for most debit disputes (extended cases up to 45 days) — ask for it explicitly.
Document everything: case number, rep name, exact time of report, what was promised in writing.
Do not narrate guesses about who did it. 'I don't recognize this charge' is sufficient; speculation can be used to deny the claim ('you authorized your roommate'?).

phrases.opener: Hi, I'm calling to report unauthorized transactions on my account. I'd like to file a fraud claim, lock the card, and request provisional credit. Can you open a case?
phrases.verify_the_rep: Before I share details, can I confirm I'm speaking with the bank's fraud department and not a transferred line? Could you give me a case or reference number I can verify against the number on the back of my card?
phrases.list_the_charges: Here are the charges I'm disputing: [date, amount, merchant], [date, amount, merchant]. I did not authorize any of these and the card has [been with me / is missing].
phrases.ask_for_provisional_credit: Under Regulation E, can you process a provisional credit while the investigation proceeds? I'd like to confirm the timing in writing.
phrases.ask_for_reg_e_form: Could you send me the written Regulation E claim form or affidavit so I can complete the formal dispute today?
phrases.escalate_to_fraud_supervisor: Got it. Could you connect me to a fraud-team supervisor or claim specialist? I want to make sure the claim is opened correctly the first time.
phrases.ask_about_card_reissue: Please cancel the current card number and issue a new one. What's the standard delivery time, and is expedited shipping available at no charge given the fraud?
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. Can you read back the case number, the provisional-credit amount and date, the new card delivery date, and your name? I'd like a written summary emailed or messaged in-app.

tactics.name: Verify the line before disclosing
tactics.when: Call connects, before reading any account info.
tactics.script: Use the `verify_the_rep` phrase. If the user reached this number via a 'fraud alert' message rather than the card back, hang up and redial the card-back number.

tactics.name: Open the claim cleanly
tactics.script: Use the `opener`. State three asks together: file the claim, lock/reissue the card, request provisional credit. Bundling sets the call's agenda.

tactics.name: List charges in a single pass
tactics.script: Read each line item with date, amount, exact merchant string. If there are more than 5, ask the rep whether they want them by email upload or by phone.

tactics.name: Pin provisional-credit timing
tactics.when: Rep confirms the claim is opened.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_provisional_credit`. For debit cards: cite Reg E 10-business-day rule. Get the rep to commit to either same-day, within 10 days, or to flag the case as 'extended investigation' (up to 45 days).

tactics.name: Escalate if first rep stalls
tactics.when: Front-line rep says 'we can only open the case, supervisor decides credit.'
tactics.script: Politely use `escalate_to_fraud_supervisor`. Fraud-team supervisors and claim specialists have authority front-line reps do not.

tactics.name: Cap the disclosure
tactics.when: Rep asks for SSN, full DOB, or 'security questions' that feel excessive.
tactics.script: Offer last 4 of SSN, last 4 of card, and answer the bank's own pre-set questions. If asked for full SSN with no clear reason, ask 'is full SSN required for this dispute, or will the last 4 work?' — usually last 4 is enough.

tactics.name: Get the writeback
tactics.when: Claim opens and credit timing is set.
tactics.script: Use `graceful_close`. Ask for the summary in-app or by secure message — never plain email with full account info.

boundaries: Do NOT call any 'fraud line' number from a text, email, or pop-up — only the card-back / official-site number.
Do NOT share full SSN unless the bank explicitly says it is required and the user has approved (use `ask_operator`).
Do NOT speculate about who made the charges — say 'I did not authorize this.'
Do NOT agree to 'verify your identity' by reading back a one-time code the bank texted you — legitimate bank reps will never ask for that code.
Do NOT close the call without a case number, rep name, and timing on provisional credit and card reissue.
success_signals: Rep opens a formal fraud case and reads back a case number.
Rep confirms provisional credit timing (same-day, 10 business days, or 45-day extended).
Rep confirms the compromised card is locked and a replacement is in the mail.
Rep offers expedited shipping at no charge.
Rep agrees to send a written summary via secure message.
failure_signals: Rep refuses to open the case until a paper form is mailed in (red flag — push for supervisor).
Rep insists on provisional credit being denied without citing a specific reason.
Caller-ID does not match the card-back number you intended to dial (you may be on a spoofed line — hang up, redial).
Rep asks for a code they just texted (this is not the bank — terminate the call).

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm case number, provisional-credit amount + date, new card delivery date and tracking, rep name. Ask for a written summary via secure in-app message. Tell the operator to monitor the account daily for the next 7 days.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Get a case number anyway, note rep name, and ask to be transferred to a fraud claim specialist. If still blocked, file a CFPB complaint and escalate via the bank's executive-relations team. Inform the operator and request approval to escalate.
follow_ups: Calendar: re-check provisional credit in 10 business days.
Calendar: re-check final investigation outcome in 45 days.
If identity theft is suspected, advise operator to consider a credit freeze at Equifax, Experian, TransUnion.

required_user_info: List of disputed charges (date, amount, merchant string)
Whether the physical card is in possession or missing
Approved mailing address for the replacement card
Operator approval threshold for any action that moves money
